terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.s1Text: By accessing or using HookSniff ("Service"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service ("Terms"). If you do not agree, do not use the Service.
terms.s2Text: HookSniff provides webhook delivery infrastructure for developers. The Service includes webhook delivery, retry logic, signature verification, monitoring dashboard, and related APIs.
s3Items: You must provide accurate registration information
You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials
You must be at least 18 years old to use the Service
One account per person or entity
s4Items: API keys are issued to your account and must be kept confidential
You are responsible for all activity that occurs under your API keys
Notify us immediately if you suspect unauthorized use of your API keys
We may rotate or revoke API keys for security reasons
s5Items: Use the Service for any unlawful purpose
Send webhooks to internal, private, or malicious endpoints (SSRF)
Attempt to circumvent rate limits or security measures
Resell or redistribute the Service without authorization
Use the Service to send spam, malware, or phishing content
Interfere with or disrupt the Service or its infrastructure
s6Items: You retain ownership of all data you send through the Service
We process webhook payloads solely for delivery purposes
We do not inspect, analyze, or sell your webhook data
Payloads are stored temporarily for delivery and retry purposes, then deleted per the retention policy
s7Items: The Service is provided on a "best effort" basis
We do not guarantee 100% delivery or uptime
Paid plans include the SLA terms specified in the applicable pricing tier
We may perform scheduled maintenance with reasonable advance notice
s8Items: Webhook delivery logs are retained according to your plan's retention period
After the retention period, data is automatically and permanently deleted
s9Items: Paid plans are billed monthly in advance
All fees are non-refundable except as required by applicable law
We may change pricing with 30 days' notice
Failure to pay may result in account suspension or downgrade to Free plan
Overage charges may apply if you exceed your plan limits
s10Items: The Service, including its code, design, and documentation, is our intellectual property
You retain all rights to your data and content
You grant us a limited license to process your data solely to provide the Service
terms.s11MaxExtent: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW:
s11Items: THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND
WE SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR ANY INDIRECT, INCIDENTAL, SPECIAL, CONSEQUENTIAL, OR PUNITIVE DAMAGES
OUR TOTAL LIABILITY SHALL NOT EXCEED THE AMOUNT YOU PAID US IN THE 12 MONTHS PRECEDING THE CLAIM
WE SHALL NOT BE LIABLE FOR DATA LOSS, LOST PROFITS, OR BUSINESS INTERRUPTION
terms.s12Intro: You agree to indemnify and hold us harmless from any claims, losses, or damages arising from:
s12Items: Your violation of any third-party rights
Webhook payloads you send through the Service
s13Items: You may terminate your account at any time through the dashboard
We may terminate or suspend your account for violation of these Terms
We may discontinue the Service with 30 days' notice
Upon termination, your data will be deleted within 30 days
terms.s14Text: We may update these Terms from time to time. We will notify you of material changes via email or dashboard notification. Continued use after changes constitutes acceptance.
terms.s15Text: These Terms are governed by the laws of Turkey. Any disputes shall be resolved in the courts of Turkey.
terms.s16Text: For questions about these Terms, contact:

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.s1Text: HookSniff ("we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your information when you use our webhook delivery service ("Service").
s21Items: Payment information (processed by Stripe, not stored by us)
s22Items: API request logs (endpoint URLs, timestamps, response codes)
Webhook delivery logs (payloads, delivery attempts, status)
IP addresses (for security and rate limiting)
s3Items: Send service-related notifications (delivery failures, billing alerts)
Improve the Service and fix bugs
s4Items: We process webhook payloads solely for delivery to your configured endpoints
Payloads are stored temporarily for delivery, retry, and logging purposes
Payloads are automatically deleted according to your plan's retention period
We do not inspect, analyze, mine, or sell your webhook data
We do not use your webhook data for advertising or profiling
privacy.s5Intro: We do NOT sell your personal data. We may share information with:
s5Items: Polar.sh: Payment processing (subject to Polar.sh's Privacy Policy)
iyzico: Payment processing for Turkish customers (subject to iyzico's Privacy Policy)
Infrastructure providers: Cloud hosting for Service operation
Legal authorities: When required by law, court order, or to protect our rights
Business transfers: In connection with a merger, acquisition, or sale of assets
s6Items: API keys are hashed before storage (SHA-256)
Access controls and authentication for all systems
privacy.s6Disclaimer: However, no method of transmission or storage is 100% secure. We cannot guarantee absolute security.
privacy.s7Note: After the retention period, data is automatically and permanently deleted.
s8Items: Access: Request a copy of your personal data
Correct: Request correction of inaccurate data
Delete: Request deletion of your personal data
Export: Request your data in a portable format
Object: Object to processing of your personal data
Withdraw consent: Where processing is based on consent
privacy.s8How: To exercise these rights, contact us via the contact form
privacy.s9Text: We use essential cookies for authentication and session management. You can control cookies through your browser settings.
privacy.s10Text: Your data may be processed in countries outside your own. We ensure appropriate safeguards are in place for international transfers, including Standard Contractual Clauses where required.
privacy.s11Text: Our Service is not intended for children under 16. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children.
privacy.s12Text: We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. We will notify you of material changes via email or dashboard notification.
privacy.s13Text: For privacy-related inquiries, contact us via the contact form

faqSubtitle: Everything you need to know about HookSniff
a1: HookSniff is a webhook delivery service for developers. We reliably deliver webhooks to your endpoints with automatic retries, HMAC signatures, and real-time monitoring. Think of it as a postal service for your HTTP callbacks.
q2: How is HookSniff different from Svix or Hookdeck?
a2: HookSniff offers similar features at 10x lower cost. Our Pro plan is $29/mo vs Svix at $490/mo. We also support 11 SDKs, FIFO delivery, CloudEvents, and a schema registry — features that competitors charge extra for or don't offer.
a3: Yes. HookSniff handles thousands of webhook deliveries daily with 99.9% uptime. We use PostgreSQL for durable storage, automatic retries with exponential backoff, and dead letter queues for failed deliveries.
a4: Sign up for a free account at hooksniff.vercel.app, get your API key, and create an endpoint. Send your first webhook in under 5 minutes with our quick start guide or SDK.
q5: Do I need a credit card to start?
a5: No. The free plan includes 10,000 webhook deliveries per month with no credit card required. Upgrade to Pro or Business when you need more.
q6: What payment methods do you accept?
a6: We accept all major credit cards via Stripe, and support Polar.sh for global payments and iyzico for Turkish customers. All plans are billed monthly.
q7: Can I change my plan at any time?
a7: Yes. Upgrade or downgrade anytime from your dashboard. Upgrades take effect immediately with prorated billing. Downgrades take effect at the end of your current billing period.
q8: What happens if I exceed my plan limits?
a8: We won't cut you off. If you exceed your webhook limit, additional deliveries are queued and processed when your limit resets. We'll notify you so you can upgrade if needed.
a9: Failed deliveries are retried with exponential backoff: 30s, 60s, 120s, 5min, 10min, 30min. After max attempts (default 5), the delivery moves to a dead letter queue for debugging.
a10: Every webhook delivery includes an X-HookSniff-Signature header containing an HMAC-SHA256 hash of the payload. Verify this signature on your end to ensure the webhook came from HookSniff and wasn't tampered with.
a11: Yes. In addition to HTTP POST delivery, HookSniff supports WebSocket connections for real-time event streaming. Configure WebSocket delivery per endpoint.
q12: What is the dead letter queue?
a12: The dead letter queue stores failed deliveries that exhausted all retry attempts. You can inspect payloads, error messages, and response codes to debug issues before replaying the delivery.
a13: Yes. All data is encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest. We use HMAC-SHA256 for webhook signatures, Argon2id for password hashing, and support 2FA via TOTP. We're SOC 2 ready.
a14: Yes, for the duration of your plan's retention period (7-365 days depending on plan). Payloads are stored encrypted and automatically deleted after the retention period. You can also delete them manually.
q15: Can I use a custom domain?
a15: Yes. Business plan customers can configure custom domains for their webhook endpoints. SSL certificates are automatically provisioned via Let's Encrypt.
cantFindAnswer: Can't find the answer you're looking for? Our support team is here to help.

getStarted.viaApiDesc: Use the SDK code from Step 3, or send a POST request to
getStarted.tipPlayground: Tip: Use HookSniff Playground to test webhooks without a real endpoint. It gives you a temporary URL that captures all requests.

pricing.title: Pricing
pricing.heroSubtitle: Save 12+ months of webhook infrastructure development. Start free, scale when ready.
pricing.heroNote: No credit card required. No surprise charges. Cancel anytime.
pricing.proDesc: Unleash your data connectivity with enterprise features and better support.
pricing.payWhatYouUse: Only pay for what you actually send
pricing.payWhatYouUseDesc: Retries are free. Filtered messages are free. Failed deliveries that we retry 5 times still count as 1 webhook. We absorb the cost because reliable delivery is our promise.
pricing.roiDesc: See how much you save with HookSniff vs competitors.
buildOwnItems: Retry logic is hard to get right
pricing.startupDesc: Enhance your webhook experience with new features and grow your startup!
pricing.ctaDesc: Start delivering webhooks reliably with HookSniff.
pricing.enterpriseDesc: Need more? Need something different? Let us know, we will build a custom plan for you.
pricing.developerDesc: Perfect for trying out HookSniff. No setup needed, free forever for side projects.

pricingFaq.a: A webhook is an event delivered to your endpoint. Failed deliveries that are retried do not count as additional webhooks — retries are free. Filtered messages are also free.

pricingFaq.q: Do I need a credit card to start?
pricingFaq.a: No. You can sign up and start sending webhooks on the Free plan without a credit card. Upgrade when you need more volume or features.

pricingFaq.q: What happens if I exceed my plan limit?
pricingFaq.a: We never drop webhooks. If you exceed your monthly limit, additional events are metered at the per-plan rate. You can also set a hard cap in your dashboard settings.
Yes. A webhook includes all its retries. If a delivery fails and we retry it 3 times, that still counts as 1 webhook. We absorb the retry cost because reliable delivery is our core promise.
Yes. Upgrades take effect immediately and are prorated. Downgrades take effect at the start of your next billing cycle. No penalties, no hidden fees.
Yes! If you're an early-stage startup (pre-Series A), contact us via the contact form with your details. We offer significant discounts and extended free tiers.

pricingFaq.q: What's the difference between Pro and Business?
pricingFaq.a: Pro is for growing teams that need more volume and better monitoring. Business is for teams that need SLA guarantees, FIFO delivery, SSO, and dedicated support.
Yes. Annual billing saves you 20% compared to monthly. That's $278/year for Pro and $950/year for Business.

pricingFaq.q: What payment methods do you accept?
pricingFaq.a: We accept all major credit cards via Stripe. For Business and Enterprise plans, we also support invoicing and bank transfers.
Yes! HookSniff is open-source. You can self-host with Docker. Self-hosted instances get community support. Paid plans add priority support for self-hosted.
We support 11 SDKs: Node.js, Python, Rust, C#, Go, Swift, PHP, Elixir, Java, Kotlin, and Ruby.
Yes. All data is encrypted in transit (TLS 1.3) and at rest. We use HMAC-SHA256 for webhook signatures, Argon2 for password hashing, and support 2FA/TOTP.
Yes. We are GDPR compliant. We process data in EU regions, support data export/deletion requests, and have a published privacy policy.
Free and Pro plans have a 99.9% uptime target (best effort). Business plans include a 99.9% uptime SLA with credits if we miss it.

pricingFaq.q: How does HookSniff compare to Svix?
pricingFaq.a: HookSniff offers similar features at 10x lower cost. Svix starts at $490/month; HookSniff Pro is $49/month. We also support more SDKs (11 vs 6), FIFO delivery, and CloudEvents.
Yes. You can export all your webhook logs, endpoint configurations, and delivery history as JSON or CSV from the dashboard.

transforms.selectEndpointHint: Select an endpoint to manage transforms
transforms.empty: No transform rules. Create one to start transforming payloads.
transforms.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this transform rule?

rateLimiting.title: ⚡ Rate Limiting
rateLimiting.subtitle: Monitor and configure rate limits for your webhook endpoints.
rateLimiting.emptyDesc: HookSniff automatically rate-limits webhook deliveries to protect your endpoints. Configure limits per endpoint in settings.
rateLimiting.tokenBucketDesc: Each endpoint has a token bucket that refills at the configured rate. Requests consume tokens.
rateLimiting.burstHandlingDesc: Short bursts are allowed up to the burst size, then requests are queued.
rateLimiting.queueRetryDesc: Excess requests are queued and delivered when capacity is available. Failed deliveries retry with exponential backoff.
rateLimiting.perEndpointConfigDesc: Each endpoint can have custom rate limits. Defaults: 10 req/sec, 600 req/min, burst 20.
rateLimiting.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the rate limit for this endpoint?

sso.title: 🔐 SSO / SAML
sso.subtitle: Configure Single Sign-On for your organization. Enterprise plan required.
sso.enableSsoDesc: When enabled, all team members must authenticate via SSO.
sso.upgradeHintSuffix: to enable SSO for your organization.

auditLog.title: 📋 Audit Log
auditLog.subtitle: Track all activity in your workspace. Who did what, when, and from where.
auditLog.noActivityDesc: Actions like login, endpoint creation, API key rotation, and team changes will appear here.

routing.title: 🔀 Routing
routing.subtitle: Configure how webhooks are routed to your endpoints. Choose between round-robin, latency-based, or failover strategies.

customDomain.title: 🌐 Custom Domain
customDomain.subtitle: Use your own domain for the webhook portal. White-label your customers' experience.
customDomain.dnsRecordsDesc: Add these records to your DNS provider (Cloudflare, Route53, etc.)
customDomain.domainAdded: Domain added! Add the DNS records below.
customDomain.step1Desc: Enter the domain you want to use (e.g., webhooks.yourcompany.com)
customDomain.step2Desc: We'll give you CNAME and TXT records to add to your DNS provider
customDomain.step3Desc: We verify ownership and automatically provision an SSL certificate

signatureVerifier.title: 🔐 Signature Verifier
signatureVerifier.subtitle: Verify webhook signatures to ensure payloads are authentic. HookSniff signs every webhook with HMAC-SHA256.
signatureVerifier.validDesc: The payload is authentic and has not been tampered with.
signatureVerifier.invalidDesc: The signature does not match. The payload may have been tampered with.
signatureVerifier.step1Desc: When a webhook is delivered, HookSniff computes an HMAC hash of the raw request body using your endpoint's secret key.
signatureVerifier.step2Desc: The signature is sent in the x-hooksniff-signature header (format: sha256=<hex_digest>).
signatureVerifier.step3Desc: Compute the same HMAC hash on your server and compare it with the received signature. Use constant-time comparison to prevent timing attacks.

endpointSettings.title: Endpoint Settings
endpointSettings.rotateSecretDesc: Rotate your signing secret. The old secret remains valid for 24 hours to allow seamless migration.
endpointSettings.testWebhookDesc: Send a test webhook to this endpoint to verify it's working correctly.
endpointSettings.testFailed: ❌ Failed — check endpoint URL and try again.
endpointSettings.rotateConfirmDesc: The old secret will remain valid for 24 hours. Update your webhook consumers to use the new secret before then.
endpointSettings.toastSecretRotated: Secret rotated! Old secret valid for 24 hours.
endpointSettings.toastTestSent: Test webhook sent! Check your endpoint.

deliveryDetail.title: Delivery Details
deliveryDetail.noAttemptDataDesc: Attempts will appear here once the delivery is processed
deliveryDetail.noDebugData: No additional debug data captured for this attempt
deliveryDetail.replayMessage: Replay this webhook delivery to the same endpoint? This will create a new delivery attempt.

templatesPage.title: 📦 Templates
templatesPage.subtitle: Pre-built webhook configurations for common use cases. Apply a template to quickly set up endpoints.
templatesPage.noTemplatesDesc: Templates will appear here once configured.
templatesPage.stripe-like-payments_desc: Standard payment event flow modeled after Stripe's webhook architecture — covers the full payment lifecycle from intent to settlement with dispute handling and fraud signals
templatesPage.shopify-like-orders_desc: Order lifecycle events modeled after Shopify's webhook system — covers the complete order journey from cart to delivery including inventory management and fulfillment tracking
templatesPage.github-like-repos_desc: Code event flow modeled after GitHub's webhook system — covers repository events, pull requests, issues, CI/CD status, and team collaboration signals
templatesPage.twilio-like-messaging_desc: Message delivery events modeled after Twilio's webhook system — covers SMS/MMS/voice delivery status, incoming messages, and carrier feedback

schemasPage.title: 📋 Schemas
schemasPage.subtitle: Define and validate event schemas. Ensure your webhooks conform to expected formats.
schemasPage.noSchemasDesc: Schemas define the structure of your webhook payloads. For example, you can specify that an 'order.created' event must include fields like order_id (string), total (number), and currency (string). Once defined, incoming webhook data is automatically validated and requests that don't match the expected format are rejected. This feature is optional — webhooks work normally without schemas.

webhookBuilder.title: 🔧 Webhook Builder
webhookBuilder.subtitle: Visually create and send webhook payloads. No code required.
webhookBuilder.noEndpoints: No endpoints found. Create an endpoint first.

apiImporter.title: 📥 API Spec Importer
apiImporter.subtitle: Import endpoints from an OpenAPI/Swagger specification. Auto-create webhook endpoints from your existing API.
apiImporter.failedToParseJson: Failed to parse. Make sure it's valid JSON.
apiImporter.tip: 💡 Tip: Imported endpoints will be created with the URL from your API spec. You can update the URL later to point to your actual webhook receiver.

portalCustomize.title: 🖼️ Portal Customization
portalCustomize.subtitle: Customize the look and feel of your embedded webhook portal.
portalCustomize.showEventsDesc: Allow users to view event types
portalCustomize.showDeliveriesDesc: Allow users to view delivery history
portalCustomize.allowedEventsDesc: Leave empty to show all events. Add specific event types to filter what users can subscribe to.
portalCustomize.embedCodeDesc: Copy this code into your dashboard to embed the portal.

retryPolicy.title: 🔄 Retry Policy
retryPolicy.subtitle: Configure global retry behavior for webhook deliveries. Per-endpoint overrides take precedence.
retryPolicy.saved: Retry policy saved for all endpoints!
retryPolicy.enableDlqDesc: Move permanently failed deliveries to DLQ
retryPolicy.retryOnStatusCodesDesc: Webhooks that return these HTTP status codes will be retried.
retryPolicy.delayPreviewDesc: How delays will look with current settings:
retryPolicy.tipContent: Per-endpoint retry policies override these global defaults. Configure them in
retryPolicy.noEndpoints: No endpoints found. Create an endpoint first to configure retry policy.

inbound.title: Inbound Webhooks
inbound.subtitle: Receive webhooks from Stripe, GitHub, Shopify and route them through HookSniff

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.subtitle: Get in touch with our team
contact.name: Your name
contact.subject: Subject
contact.messageSent: ✅ Message sent! We'll get back to you soon.
contact.sendError: ❌ Failed to send. Please try again or reach us on GitHub Discussions.

about.title: About HookSniff
about.cta: Join developers who trust HookSniff for their webhook delivery.
about.heroSubtitle: Reliable webhook delivery infrastructure built by developers, for developers.
about.missionP1: Webhooks are the backbone of modern integrations, but building reliable webhook infrastructure is hard. We built HookSniff to make it simple — send webhooks, we deliver them. If they fail, we retry.
about.missionP2: We believe developers shouldn't have to pay enterprise prices for reliable webhook delivery. That's why HookSniff starts at $0/month with a generous free tier.
about.storyP1: HookSniff started as a side project in 2026. We were tired of dealing with unreliable webhook deliveries and expensive third-party services. So we built our own.
about.storyP2: Today, HookSniff handles webhook delivery for developers worldwide, with support for HTTP, WebSocket, gRPC, and SQS delivery methods. Our infrastructure runs entirely on free-tier services, keeping costs at $0/month while maintaining enterprise-grade reliability.
about.storyP3: We're committed to keeping HookSniff affordable and accessible. As we grow, we'll continue to offer a generous free tier and transparent pricing.
about.securityFirstDesc: HMAC-SHA256 signatures, TLS encryption, and zero-knowledge payload processing.
about.transparentPricingDesc: No hidden fees, no surprise charges. What you see is what you pay.
about.globalInfrastructureDesc: Deployed in EU (Frankfurt) with CDN coverage worldwide via Cloudflare.

error.title: Something went wrong
error.unexpected: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
error.unexpectedError: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
error.authFailed: Authentication failed. Please try logging in again.

blog.title: Blog
blog.searchPlaceholder: Search posts by title or content...
blog.desc: Insights on webhooks, event-driven architecture, AI agents, and developer tools.
blog.post1Title: HookSniff vs Svix vs Hookdeck vs Hook0: 2026 Webhook Service Comparison
blog.post1Excerpt: A comprehensive comparison of the top webhook services in 2026 — pricing, SDKs, features, self-hosting, and free tiers. Find the right fit for your stack.
blog.post2Excerpt: Blog launch, 11/11 SDKs published, CSP fixes, 4 new DB tables, API deploy automation, admin dashboard, and 1,378 tests passing.
blog.post3Excerpt: As AI agents become autonomous, they need real-time event delivery. Webhooks are the nervous system of the agent ecosystem.
blog.post4Title: Building an MCP-Ready Webhook Service: Lessons from HookSniff
blog.post4Excerpt: MCP assumes synchronous request-response, but the real world is async. Here is how we built webhook infrastructure that bridges the gap for AI agents.
blog.post5Title: How to Handle Google Gemini Webhooks
blog.post5Excerpt: Google just added webhooks to the Gemini API. Here is how to receive, verify, and process them with HookSniff.
blog.post6Title: Complete Webhook Integration Tutorial: From Zero to Production
blog.post6Excerpt: A hands-on guide to integrating webhooks with HookSniff — from signing up and creating endpoints to verifying signatures and monitoring delivery.
blog.post7Excerpt: Stripe sends dozens of event types. Learn how to set up, verify, and handle Stripe webhooks reliably in production.
blog.post8Title: Inside HookSniff: How We Built a $0/Month Webhook Service
blog.post8Excerpt: A technical deep-dive into HookSniff architecture — Rust/Axum, PostgreSQL queues, Upstash Redis rate limiting, and how we handle 10K+ webhooks on free tiers.
blog.post9Excerpt: Blog launch, CSP fixes, 4 new database tables, API deploy automation, and 11 SDK updates.
blog.post10Title: What the Shopify Webhook Incident Teaches Us About Resilience
blog.post10Excerpt: Analysis of the Shopify webhook delivery latency incident (April 28, 2026) and lessons for building resilient webhook infrastructure.
blog.post11Excerpt: We built HookSniff to solve a simple problem — webhook delivery should just work. No more missed events, no more complex retry logic.
blog.post12Excerpt: Learn how to implement secure, reliable webhooks with HMAC signatures, idempotency keys, and exponential backoff retries.
blog.post13Excerpt: Event ordering is critical for many workflows. Learn how HookSniff guarantees ordered delivery with sequence numbers.
blog.post14Title: How an E-Commerce Platform Scaled Webhook Delivery with HookSniff
blog.post14Excerpt: How an e-commerce platform processing 50K orders/day achieved 99.97% webhook delivery rate and cut infrastructure engineering time by 60%.
blog.post15Title: How to Set Up GitHub Webhooks
blog.post15Excerpt: Receive push, PR, and issue events from GitHub. Full guide with code examples in Node.js and Python.
blog.post16Excerpt: CloudEvents v1.0 provides a standardized way to describe event data. Here is why we adopted it and how it benefits your integrations.
blog.post17Excerpt: HMAC signatures, replay attack prevention, IP whitelisting, and TLS — everything you need to secure your webhook endpoints.

changelog.title: Changelog
changelog.desc: See what's new in HookSniff — product updates, SDK releases, and infrastructure improvements.

compare.title: Compare
compare.trustedBy: A modern alternative to building your own webhooks

security.title: Security
security.heroSubtitle: Security is not optional. Every webhook is signed, every connection is encrypted, and every action is logged.
security.featureTlsDesc: All data encrypted in transit with TLS 1.3. No HTTP, no exceptions.
security.featureHmacDesc: Every webhook is signed with your secret. Verify authenticity with standard HMAC-SHA256.
security.feature2faDesc: Two-factor authentication via authenticator apps. Protect your account beyond passwords.
security.featureSsoDesc: Enterprise single sign-on. Integrate with Okta, Auth0, Google Workspace, and more.
security.featureIpWhitelistDesc: Restrict API access to specific IPs or CIDR ranges. Block unauthorized sources.
security.featureSsrfDesc: Built-in Server-Side Request Forgery protection. Block internal network access.
security.featureArgon2Desc: Industry-leading password hashing with Argon2id. No plaintext, no weak hashes.
security.featureAuditLogsDesc: Track every action: who did what, when. Full audit trail for compliance.
security.featureEuDataDesc: Data processed in eu-central-1 (Frankfurt). GDPR compliant by design.
security.featureKeyRotationDesc: Rotate API keys without downtime. Old keys invalidated instantly.
security.featureRateLimitDesc: Per-key rate limiting prevents abuse. Configurable per plan.
security.featureSecretRotationDesc: Rotate webhook secrets without breaking existing integrations. Dual-secret support.
security.gdprDesc: EU data processing, data export/deletion, DPA available
security.soc2Desc: Security controls in place, Type 1 audit planned
security.standardWebhooksDesc: Open standard for webhook signatures and delivery
security.cloudeventsDesc: CNCF standard for event data interoperability
security.responsibleDisclosureDesc: Found a security vulnerability? We appreciate responsible disclosure. Please report with details.
security.responsibleDisclosureCommit: We commit to acknowledging reports within 24 hours and providing a fix timeline within 72 hours.
security.ctaDesc: Our team is happy to discuss your security requirements.

startups.title: Startups
startups.subtitle: Special pricing for early-stage startups. Focus on your product, not webhook infrastructure.
startups.benefit50Desc: Pre-Series A startups get Pro plan for $14.50/mo (instead of $29).
startups.benefitPriorityDesc: Direct Slack channel with our engineering team for onboarding help.
startups.qualify4: Building a product (not a side project)
startups.ctaDesc: Tell us about your startup. We usually respond within 24 hours.

whatIsWebhook.title: What is a Webhook?
whatIsWebhook.subtitle: A complete guide to webhooks — how they work, why they matter, and how to use them.
whatIsWebhook.pizzaWebhookDesc: = The pizza place calls YOU when your pizza is ready.
whatIsWebhook.pizzaPollingDesc: = You keep calling the pizza place asking "Is my pizza ready?"
whatIsWebhook.step1Desc: You tell a service "When something happens, send data to this URL."
whatIsWebhook.step2Desc: A payment succeeds, a user signs up, an order ships.
whatIsWebhook.step3: The service sends a POST request
whatIsWebhook.step3Desc: It sends the event data (payload) to your URL.
whatIsWebhook.step4Desc: You receive the data and take action (update database, send email, etc.).
whatIsWebhook.ucPaymentDesc: Stripe sends a webhook when a payment succeeds or fails.
whatIsWebhook.ucCiCdDesc: GitHub sends a webhook when code is pushed, triggering a build.
whatIsWebhook.ucChatbotsDesc: Slack/Discord sends a webhook when a message is posted.
whatIsWebhook.ucEcommerceDesc: Order created, shipped, delivered — each triggers a webhook.
whatIsWebhook.ucAiDesc: An AI agent sends a webhook when a task completes.
whatIsWebhook.ucMonitoringDesc: An alert system sends a webhook when a server goes down.
whatIsWebhook.securityDesc: Webhooks are sent over HTTP, so anyone can send a request to your URL. You need to verify that the request actually came from the expected service.
whatIsWebhook.gettingStartedDesc: The easiest way to start with webhooks:
whatIsWebhook.gsStep1: Create an endpoint on your server (a URL that accepts POST requests)
whatIsWebhook.gsStep2: Register that URL with the service that will send webhooks
whatIsWebhook.gsStep3: Verify the webhook signature in your endpoint
whatIsWebhook.gsStep4: Process the event data and take action
whatIsWebhook.gsStep5: Return a 200 status code to acknowledge receipt
whatIsWebhook.proTipDesc: Use a webhook service like HookSniff to handle retries, security, and monitoring — so you can focus on your product.
whatIsWebhook.ctaDesc: HookSniff makes webhook delivery simple. Start free.
whatIsWebhook.pizzaWebhook: Pizza webhook: when you order, the kitchen gets an automatic notification

playgroundPublic.title: Playground
playgroundPublic.subtitle: Get a unique URL, send webhooks, inspect requests in real-time. No signup required.
playgroundPublic.sendToUrl: Send a webhook to your URL above
playgroundPublic.idleDesc: Get a unique URL that captures any HTTP request. Inspect headers, body, and signatures.
playgroundPublic.freeForever: Free forever · No signup · Works with any service
playgroundPublic.worksAnyServiceDesc: Stripe, GitHub, Shopify, Slack — if it sends HTTP requests, this captures it.
playgroundPublic.secureDefaultDesc: HTTPS included. Signatures captured. Data expires in 24 hours automatically.
playgroundPublic.realtimeDesc: Polls every 2 seconds. New requests appear instantly in the list.
playgroundPublic.likeSignup: Like what you see? Sign up and send webhooks for real.

cta: Join developers who trust HookSniff for their webhook delivery.

emailVerification.sent: Verification email sent! Check your inbox.

alternatives.migratingFrom: If you're migrating from {alternative}, HookSniff is the closest open-source alternative with active development, more SDKs, and managed hosting.
alternatives.svixTitle: HookSniff vs Svix — Why Choose HookSniff
alternatives.svixDesc: Looking for a Svix alternative? HookSniff offers similar features at 10x lower cost, with more SDKs and unique features like FIFO delivery.
alternatives.hookdeckTitle: HookSniff vs Hookdeck — Why Choose HookSniff
alternatives.hookdeckDesc: Looking for a Hookdeck alternative? HookSniff is open-source, self-hosted, and offers FIFO delivery and CloudEvents support.
alternatives.hook0Title: HookSniff vs Hook0 — Why Choose HookSniff
alternatives.hook0Desc: Looking for a Hook0 alternative? HookSniff offers more SDKs, better retry logic, and a production-ready managed platform.
alternatives.convoyTitle: HookSniff vs Convoy — Why Choose HookSniff
alternatives.convoyDesc: Looking for a Convoy alternative? Convoy's GitHub repository is no longer actively maintained. HookSniff offers active development and managed hosting.
alternatives.webhookRelayTitle: HookSniff vs Webhook Relay — Why Choose HookSniff
alternatives.webhookRelayDesc: Looking for a Webhook Relay alternative? HookSniff offers a modern developer experience with SDKs, retries, and real-time monitoring.
alternatives.svixAlternativesDesc: Looking for Svix alternatives? Compare HookSniff, Hookdeck, Hook0, and Convoy.
alternatives.svixAlternativesIntro: Looking for a webhook service but Svix is too expensive? Here are the best alternatives compared honestly — features, pricing, and trade-offs.
alternatives.hookdeckAlternativesDesc: Looking for Hookdeck alternatives? Compare HookSniff, Svix, Hook0, and Convoy.
alternatives.hookdeckAlternativesIntro: Looking for a webhook service but Hookdeck doesn't fit? Here are the best alternatives compared honestly — features, pricing, and trade-offs.
alternatives.bestForStartupsDesc: Teams that want zero-cost webhook infrastructure with FIFO ordering and broad SDK coverage.
alternatives.bestForRoutingDesc: Teams that need advanced event filtering, transformation, and multi-destination routing.
alternatives.bestForEuropeDesc: Teams in Europe that need a GDPR-native, open-source, self-hosted solution.
alternatives.bestForGoDesc: Go teams that want a lightweight, self-hosted webhook service written in their language.
alternatives.whenToChooseSvixDesc: Svix is the better choice if you need a battle-tested enterprise solution with SOC 2 Type 2 certification and a 99.99% uptime SLA. Svix has been around longer and has more enterprise trust. If compliance certifications and proven reliability at scale are your top priorities, Svix is a strong option — even at a higher price point.
alternatives.whenToChooseHookdeckDesc: Hookdeck excels at complex event routing with filtering and transformation rules. Their 99.999% uptime SLA is the highest in the industry. If you need advanced event routing, SOC 2 Type 2 certification, and are okay with usage-based pricing, Hookdeck is a solid choice.
alternatives.whenToChooseHook0Desc: Hook0 is ideal if you want a fully open-source, European-built webhook service. It's a good fit for teams that prefer self-hosting and want GDPR-native data processing. The smaller feature set can be an advantage if you prefer simplicity over breadth.
alternatives.whenToChooseConvoyDesc: Convoy is a good fit for Go-native teams that want a lightweight, self-hosted webhook service. If your entire stack is Go and you prefer minimal dependencies, Convoy's single-language focus can be an advantage.
alternatives.whenToChooseWebhookRelayDesc: Webhook Relay is useful if you need webhook forwarding/tunneling for local development or simple relay use cases. It serves a different niche than full webhook delivery platforms.
alternatives.switchCta: Get the same features at 10x lower cost. Start free, scale when ready.
